title: Maintenance + re-review cadence + licence re-verification plan; tag v1.0
context: After the pilot report (task 402), the project needs a documented maintenance and re-review plan before tagging v1.0. Science guidance changes, licences change, and the oncology landscape evolves. The plan must commit a named maintenance owner, a re-review cadence for patient-facing content, and a licence re-verification schedule. Only then is the project ready to tag v1.0 and declare the donated-lane work complete.
objective: Document and commit the maintenance plan, re-review cadence for patient-facing content, and licence re-verification schedule — and tag v1.0 of the project once the plan is approved.
acceptanceCriteria: A named maintenance owner (individual or organisation) is committed and documented in the governance plan.

A content re-review cadence is defined: patient-facing modules and worksheets re-reviewed at least annually by the oncologist + advocate reviewers, or on material guideline change (whichever is sooner); process and triggers documented.

A licence re-verification schedule is defined: all sources.yaml entries re-verified by the licence steward at least annually; process for handling licences that change (e.g. NC restriction added) documented.
Issue templates for corrections, outdated-science reports, and licence-change flags are committed to the repo.
CONTRIBUTING guide is updated to document the corrections and re-review process.
v1.0 is tagged in git after plan is approved and pilot report (task 402) is published.
Maintainer + Licence steward review sign-off recorded.
output: A maintenance and sustainability plan document (governance/maintenance-plan.md) committed to the repo, with re-review and licence re-verification cadences, issue templates, and the v1.0 git tag.
